response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and web-oriented technologies, with no substantive exposure to Java enterprise frameworks (Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate), Kotlin, or JVM internals. Java appears briefly in his skills list and at one role (robotic arms and mobile orchestration tooling at Altom), but this is far removed from the deep JVM expertise, Spring ecosystem proficiency, and IDE subsystem ownership the role demands. His National Instruments role involved a C-language IDE rather than JVM tooling, and there is no evidence of experience with enterprise build tools (Gradle/Maven at depth), application server integration, or JVM-based language tooling.
strengths: Consistent software craftsmanship practices across roles: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and clean architecture.
Hands-on developer tooling experience, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at his current role.
Brief but real IDE-adjacent exposure from work on NI LabWindows/CVI, demonstrating some awareness of developer tooling product context.
concerns: Primary technical stack is TypeScript/Node.js with no enterprise Java or Kotlin work at meaningful scale or depth.
Zero mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM-based enterprise framework across the entire employment history.
No demonstrated knowledge of JVM internals, advanced build tooling (Gradle/Maven), or application server integration.

key_factors.factor: No Spring or JVM framework experience
